summary: Frontend bug-fix verification: category icons on /dashboard timeline now render as real inline <svg> lucide-react elements for all required categories (Makanan, Hiburan, Rental, Transportasi, Pendidikan, Kesehatan, Freelance, Gaji, Bonus, Lain-lain). Verified in both LIGHT and DARK modes via [data-testid='theme-toggle-btn']. No empty <i data-lucide> tags remain. Regression checks passed on nav, add transaction, calendar, analysis, and pro pages.

issues: DOMPET field uses native <select> (Tunai) instead of shadcn Select — inconsistent with rest of shadcn UI (minor, pre-existing, not part of this bug fix scope)

regression_checks.dashboard_loads: PASS - Rp 22.925.000 total saldo, 15 tx rendered
regression_checks.add_transaction_page: PASS - renders shadcn calendar popover trigger showing 'Kamis, 16 Juli 2026'
regression_checks.dark_mode_toggle: PASS - .dark class applied to <html>, icons remain visible with category colors

critical_code_review_comments: categories.js ICON_MAP correctly maps kebab-case icon strings to lucide-react components with a CircleDashed fallback via getCategoryIcon() — clean & robust
Minor: /app/frontend/src/pages/TransactionAddPage (Catat Transaksi) still uses native HTML <select> for DOMPET dropdown; consider shadcn Select for UI consistency (pre-existing, non-blocking)
seed_data_creation: None (used existing reviewer seed with 15 transactions)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug fix verified. Login flow: landing page requires clicking 'Mulai Sekarang' before reaching email/password form. Bottom nav testids: nav-dashboard, nav-calendar, nav-analysis, nav-add-fab, nav-profile (note: nav-profile was NOT FOUND in DOM — may need to be added if profile screen is expected). Routes are Indonesian: /kalender, /analisis, /tambah, /pro.
rca of the issue: Previously the timeline used '<i data-lucide={cat.icon}>' which required a global lucide.createIcons() runtime — since app uses lucide-react (component-based), the <i> tags were never hydrated into SVGs, producing empty boxes. Fix in DashboardPage.jsx uses `const CatIcon = getCategoryIcon(cat.icon)` then `<CatIcon />` — real React component rendered as inline <svg>. Verified working in light & dark mode.
